cuisine: Chinese Herbal and Functional Food Ingredient
region_of_origin: China; widely used across Singapore, Malaysia, and Southeast Asia
food_subgroup: Sugar and sweeteners — crystallised sucrose; nutritionally identical to white granulated sugar

preparation_method: slow crystallisation from dissolved cane or beet sugar solution

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 5g (one small lump or one level teaspoon equivalent), providing about 19 kcal and 5g of sugar.

nutrient_source.title: Sugars, granulated — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 19335)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Rock sugar (冰糖) is crystallised sucrose — chemically and nutritionally identical to granulated white sugar (sucrose). USDA SR Legacy entry for granulated sugar (FDC ID 19335) is the exact nutritional equivalent. Values confirmed consistent with input record: 387 kcal/100g, 99.8g carbohydrate, 0g protein, 0g fat.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — sucrose entry; University of Sydney GI Database — sucrose
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: Rock sugar is 100% sucrose. Sucrose GI is established at approximately 65 (medium) in the University of Sydney GI Database and confirmed in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). GL per 5g serving = (65 × 4.99g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 3.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content (2mg/100g); however, excess sugar intake elevates cardiovascular risk via triglyceride elevation
hypertension.guidance: Rock sugar contains essentially no sodium (2mg/100g) and is not a direct hypertension concern, but patients with hypertension and comorbid metabolic syndrome should limit all added sugars including rock sugar to reduce triglyceride-mediated cardiovascular risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: 100% sucrose with GI ~65; nutritionally identical to white sugar; commonly misperceived as a 'healthier' traditional sweetener in Singapore Chinese culture
type2_diabetes.guidance: Rock sugar is crystallised sucrose (GI ~65, 4 kcal/g) and is nutritionally identical to white granulated sugar — patients with Type 2 Diabetes must be counselled that rock sugar in herbal soups, barley water, chrysanthemum tea, and bird's nest provides the same glycaemic load as equivalent white sugar, and three such servings per day can contribute 15–45g of added sugar, exceeding the WHO/HPB free sugar limit of 25g/day.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High fructose load from sucrose (50% fructose) promotes hepatic de novo lipogenesis, elevated triglycerides, and increased cardiovascular risk with habitual excess intake
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Habitual consumption of rock sugar in multiple daily Chinese herbal preparations contributes significant fructose load (sucrose is 50% fructose), which promotes elevated serum triglycerides and hepatic fat accumulation — patients with cardiovascular disease should minimise all added sucrose sources including rock sugar and consider stevia or low-GI alternatives.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High dietary sugar promotes systemic inflammation; fructose component increases uric acid production
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: High sugar intake from rock sugar promotes systemic inflammation and the fructose component of sucrose increases uric acid production, which may worsen gout and inflammatory arthritis — patients with rheumatoid arthritis should reduce all forms of added sugar including rock sugar.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Rock sugar (冰糖, bīng táng) is crystallised sucrose produced by dissolving refined cane or beet sugar and allowing it to recrystallise slowly into large, pale amber or white translucent lumps. It is chemically and nutritionally identical to white granulated sugar: 387 kcal per 100g, 99.8g carbohydrate (all sucrose), 0g protein, 0g fat, GI approximately 65 (medium). The crystallisation process creates a visually distinctive, slower-dissolving form of sucrose but does not alter its sugar content, caloric density, glycaemic index, or metabolic impact in any way. In Singapore and across the Chinese diaspora, rock sugar is ubiquitous as a sweetener in Chinese sweet soups (tong sui), barley water (薏米水), red date and longan soup, bird's nest (燕窝), chrysanthemum tea (菊花茶), and herbal teas. It is also used in Hokkien-style braised pork (lor bak) and char siu. A critical nutritional misconception prevalent in Singapore Chinese culture is that rock sugar is 'more natural', 'cooling', 'less processed', or 'healthier' than white sugar — this belief is not supported by nutritional science. CLINICAL SIGNIFICANCE: Patients who consume 3–5 servings of rock-sugar-sweetened preparations daily may ingest 30–75g of added sugar from this category alone, far exceeding the WHO and HPB recommendation of less than 25g free sugar per day. The practical counselling message is: rock sugar equals white sugar in every nutritionally relevant respect; minimise in all preparations regardless of cultural context.
common_user_queries: is rock sugar better for health
rock sugar herbal soup sugar content
meal_context: braised pork (lor bak, char siu) glaze ingredient

confidence_reason: Rock sugar is 100% sucrose — an exact match to USDA SR Legacy granulated sugar (FDC ID 19335). All macronutrient values are confirmed by this approved primary source. GI of 65 for sucrose is well-established in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature. No estimation or analogy was required.
notes: protein_g is naturally zero: rock sugar is 100% crystallised sucrose and contains no protein by composition, confirmed per USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 19335. fat_g is naturally zero: rock sugar contains no fat by composition, confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are all confirmed zero as this is a pure carbohydrate food with no lipid fraction. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero: sucrose contains no dietary fibre. All vitamin values are confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy — pure sucrose contains no vitamins. water_g set to 0.2g based on USDA SR Legacy (granulated sugar moisture ~0.02–0.2g/100g). No -1 sentinel values used as USDA SR Legacy provides complete nutrient data for this food. GL per serving calculated as: (65 × 4.99g available carbohydrate per 5g serving) / 100 = 3.2. IMPORTANT CULTURAL NOTE: The widespread belief in Singapore Chinese culture that rock sugar is healthier than white sugar is a critical nutritional misconception — both are 100% sucrose with identical GI, caloric density, and metabolic impact.
